title: Mathematical Encryption Model – Affine Cipher Single-Page App

requirements.text: Create a single frontend file (frontend/public/index.html or equivalent static entry) containing a fully self-contained single-page app titled 'Mathematical Encryption Model' with all CSS in a <style> tag and all logic in a <script> tag — no external JS libraries, no build tools, works via file:// protocol.

quotes: Everything in one index.html file
Inline CSS in <style> tag
Inline JS in <script> tag
No build tools, no frameworks
The app must work by opening the HTML file directly in a browser (file:// protocol)

acceptanceCriteria: Opening the file directly in a browser (file://) requires no server and loads without errors.
No external JS or CSS libraries are referenced.
All styles are inside a <style> tag and all logic inside a <script> tag.

requirements.text: Implement affine cipher encryption using E(x) = (3x + 5) mod 26 and decryption using D(x) = 9(x − 5) mod 26. Only A–Z and a–z are transformed; spaces, digits, and punctuation pass through unchanged. Original letter case must be preserved in the output.

quotes: Encryption: E(x) = (3x + 5) mod 26
Decryption: D(x) = 9(x − 5) mod 26
Only letters (A-Z, a-z) are encrypted; spaces, numbers, and punctuation pass through unchanged.
Preserve original letter case in output.

acceptanceCriteria: Encrypting 'Hello World!' produces the correct affine cipher output with 'H', 'W', '!' and space handled correctly.
Decrypting the encrypted output returns the original plaintext with matching case.
Non-letter characters appear unchanged in both encrypted and decrypted results.

requirements.text: Build the interface layout with five sections: (1) Header with title 'Mathematical Encryption Model' and a subtle subtitle; (2) Input Card with a textarea labeled 'Enter Message'; (3) Button Row with 'Encrypt' and 'Decrypt' buttons side by side; (4) Output Card with two sub-sections — 'Encrypted Text' and 'Decrypted Text' — each showing a result field with a copy-to-clipboard button; (5) Explanation Panel as an accordion with four entries: letter-to-number mapping (A=0…Z=25), modular arithmetic overview, why 9 is the modular inverse of 3 mod 26 (3×9=27≡1 mod 26), and how encryption protects digital data.

quotes: Header: Title 'Mathematical Encryption Model' with a subtle subtitle
Input Card: Textarea labeled 'Enter Message'
Button Row: Encrypt button and Decrypt button side by side
Output Card: Two sections — 'Encrypted Text' and 'Decrypted Text' — each with a copyable result field
Explanation Panel: An info/accordion section with four explanations

acceptanceCriteria: All five layout sections are visible and correctly ordered on page load.
Clicking Encrypt populates the 'Encrypted Text' result field.
Clicking Decrypt populates the 'Decrypted Text' result field.
Each result field has a working copy-to-clipboard button.
The accordion items expand/collapse individually when clicked.
All four explanation entries are present with correct mathematical content.

requirements.text: Apply a minimal academic design: white cards on a light gray-blue background, accent color deep navy or indigo, rounded corners (border-radius), soft box shadows on cards, easy-to-read typography (Inter via Google Fonts with system-font fallback), subtle hover animations on buttons, and a subtle reveal animation when results appear. Ensure full mobile responsiveness with appropriate max-width media queries.

quotes: Minimal and academic aesthetic
Card layout with soft box shadows
Rounded corners (border-radius)
Easy-to-read fonts (use Google Fonts: Inter or system fonts as fallback)
Color palette: white cards on a light gray/blue background, accent color deep navy or indigo
Subtle animations on button hover and result reveal
Fully responsive for mobile screens (max-width media queries)

acceptanceCriteria: Cards display with visible rounded corners and box shadows.
Background is light gray-blue; cards are white; primary accent color is deep navy or indigo.
Buttons show a visible hover transition (color shift or shadow change).
Result fields animate in (e.g., fade or slide) when populated.
Layout is usable and unclipped on a 375px-wide mobile viewport.
Inter font loads from Google Fonts; a system-font stack is specified as fallback.

constraints: No external JavaScript libraries — pure vanilla JS only.
No build tools or frameworks; the file must work via file:// protocol without a server.
All CSS and JS must be inline within the single HTML file.
Google Fonts (Inter) may be loaded via a <link> tag in <head> since it is a font resource, not a JS dependency.
nonGoals: Backend canister or Motoko logic — this is a fully static frontend-only app.
Support for cipher parameters other than a=3, b=5.
Server-side rendering or a build pipeline.
